name: social-story-vertical
description: 9:16 vertical social media story (1080×1920, 8s). Dark background with gradient color wash, large hook text at top that scale-bounces in fast, supporting text below with staggered entrance, a decorative circle accent, and a slow background scale drift. All content kept within mobile-safe center 80% zone. Fast Tech/SaaS energy (0.7× timing multiplier). Cyberpunk neon palette.

hook_text.description: Primary hook text at top — short, bold, max 4 words per line for vertical legibility

accent_color.description: Neon Magenta #FF2DAA for hook text and accent circle

bg_color.description: Deep navy base #0B1020 from Laser Lagoon palette

steps.note: 9:16 vertical (1080×1920) at 29.97fps. Deep navy #0B1020 — the Laser Lagoon base for maximum neon vibrancy against the dark. Store returned ID as $compId.
Explicit background solid. This is the lowest layer — all other elements composite above it.

Gradient Ramp on the background solid creates a dynamic vertical gradient from deep navy to near-black — establishes depth and makes the center feel lighter (drawing the eye inward). This is a native AE effect, no plugins needed.
Gradient starts at top-center (540, 0) — vertical top-to-bottom gradient at 90° angle for 'stability, strength' per gradient angle guide.
Gradient ends at bottom-center — full vertical span of the 1920px frame.
Start color #15113F — slightly lighter, more saturated navy/indigo at the top. Creates a subtle indigo-to-black depth gradient.
End color #030A0A — near-pure black at the bottom. The lighter-top/darker-bottom gradient pulls the eye upward toward the hook text.

Subtle background scale drift: 100%→110% over 8s — Ken Burns-style slow zoom on the gradient BG. Creates a living, breathing feel without distraction. 10% over 8s = 1.25% per second, virtually imperceptible moment-to-moment but noticeable as a motion quality boost.
easeInOutSine on the background drift — 'cinematic, languid' easing matching the slow-scale character. The gentlest easing curve for an almost imperceptible acceleration.
Decorative accent circle at dead center of the frame. Will be styled as a large, thin-stroked ring for visual interest without blocking content. Positioned at comp center (540, 960).

700px diameter ring with 2px Neon Magenta stroke, no fill — a large circle outline purely for compositional geometry. 700px ≈ 65% of frame width (1080×0.65≈700), large enough to feel structural, small enough not to crowd content.
Circle is present for the full duration — it's a static compositional element, not an animated entrance.

Counter-drift on circle (95%→105%) — opposite direction to the background scale drift. This creates subtle parallax depth: background zooms in while circle ring breathes outward, adding perceived Z-depth in a 2D comp.
Same easeInOutSine as background drift — matching the rhythmic, organic pacing of the counter-drift.

100pt for vertical format — research: 'scale type up 30–40%' for 9:16 vs 16:9. This translates to ~75pt at 1080p 16:9 equivalent, scaled to 100pt. Position y=480 places hook text in the upper third of the 1920px frame (480/1920=25% from top) — within mobile-safe zone (top safe starts at 192px from top, 10%). Neon Magenta fills, all-caps tracking 50 (lower than max since font is already large). Line height 1.1 for tight hero-title stacking.
Hook text visible from frame 0 — it's the primary anchor of the story, never exits.

Fast scale-bounce entrance: 0→112%→95%→100%. Duration: 0.38s total — standard 0.5s × 0.7 Tech/SaaS multiplier = 0.35s ≈ 0.38s. Overshoot of 12% at 0.21s = within the standard 10–15% overshoot range. Decreasing intervals: 0.21s, 0.09s, 0.08s — matches spring physics decay. This hits within the 0.3–0.5s 'fast-paced entrance' spec.
easeOutExpo-equivalent: snappiest deceleration for a pop entrance. The hook text must feel immediate and attention-grabbing — this is the first thing the viewer sees.

48pt in Electric Cyan — research secondary title at 36–54px is the right zone. Position y=1280 (1280/1920=66.7%) — exactly at the lower two-thirds mark. Cyan vs. Magenta hook text creates the primary/secondary contrast hierarchy. Tracking +30 for SemiBold at this size.

Supporting text 1 starts at 0.4s — staggered 0.4s after hook text. Research: hero elements stagger at 8–16 frames (267–533ms); 0.4s = ~12 frames @ 29.97fps, the midpoint. Ensures the hook text registers first before supporting info appears.

Slide up from y=1320 to y=1280 (40px upward). Duration 0.25s — snappy sub-0.3s entrance. 40px displacement is conservative (within safe zone for legibility during motion). Combined with opacity below for a fluid, material-feeling entrance.
Simultaneous opacity 0→100 with the position slide up. The combination of position + opacity creates the Material Design 'emphasized decelerate' feel — enters from slightly below, arrives sharp.
easeOut on slide-up position — enters fast, settles smoothly. Matching Material Design 3 Emphasized Decelerate curve: starts nearly vertical, coasts to rest.
easeOut on opacity matches the position easing — both properties arrive at the same rate, avoiding any visual lag between the move and the fade-in.

CTA text in off-white #F5F5F5 — slightly smaller (42pt) and lighter weight (Medium) than Supporting Text 1 (48pt SemiBold), creating a three-tier visual hierarchy: Hook (100pt Black Magenta) → Supporting (48pt SemiBold Cyan) → CTA (42pt Medium White). Position y=1380 (71.9% of 1920px), within the 80% safe zone (1920×0.9=1728px limit).

CTA starts at 0.65s — 0.25s after Supporting Text 1 (which starts at 0.4s). Progressive stagger: 0s hook, 0.4s support 1, 0.65s CTA. Matches research 'progressive stagger' technique: slightly accelerating intervals for natural cascade.
Same slide-up pattern as Supporting Text 1 (40px from 1420→1380) at 0.23s duration — slightly faster than its predecessor per the progressive stagger acceleration principle.
Matching opacity fade for CTA text, synchronized with the position slide. Parallel opacity + position animation is the standard material-feel entrance.
Consistent easeOut on CTA slide-up — maintains the same kinetic character as Supporting Text 1 for visual cohesion.
Consistent easeOut on CTA opacity.
Electric Violet #7B2CFF solid as a Screen-mode color wash overlay. This adds a subtle violet hue to highlights of the background, creating the aurora/nebula depth from the Neon Galaxy Pulse palette.

Screen blending mode: 1-(1-A)(1-B). Only lightens — on a dark background this creates a gentle violet color tint in the lighter areas without affecting shadows. Opacity will be reduced to keep it subtle.
Hold opacity at 8% — extremely subtle. Screen at 8% opacity on a violet solid over near-black creates just a whisper of color warmth in the gradient. Too much would overpower the neon text colors.
Move the gradient color wash below the accent circle and text layers, but above the BG base — ensures the color tint affects the background but doesn't visually interfere with text.
Motion blur on hook text — the fast scale-bounce pop benefits from MB, making the entrance feel photographic and kinetic rather than digital.
Motion blur on Supporting Text 1 — the slide-up entrance will have natural vertical smear.
Motion blur on CTA text — consistent quality across all animated text layers.
180° shutter angle — cinematic film standard. 16 samples at 29.97fps provides clean, smooth motion blur without overly long render times.
